- User asked Claude Code to bring up the Jobber-OptimoRoute automation project from recent work (technician-route-automation).
- Claude Code had completed a supervised manual single-day route run for Friday 2026-07-10: fetched correct techs from Jobber → created 90 unique OptimoRoute orders → optimized all 90 stops → wrote optimized times back to Jobber board.
- Result: 90/90 Jobber visits updated with optimized start times (+3h window); 0 unassigned stops, 0 tech reassignments. Three techs' routes finalized: Cammeron (34 stops, 06:48–15:06), Cory (27 stops, 07:04–14:50), Luke (29 stops, 07:13–14:02).
- Previously the board ran stale times to 7:39pm; optimized routes now finish by ~3pm.

- Reference file saved: `projects/briefs/technician-route-automation/2026-07-10_optimized-routes-REVIEW.md`.
- Key learning identified and documented: using Jobber job number as OptimoRoute order ID is unsafe for recurring customers (dragged two other-date orders onto 07-10 during first attempt). Safe pattern: unique per-day order IDs + geocoding via `create_order` endpoint.
- Scheduled automation remained OFF throughout—this was a manual, supervised run only; no changes to the paused parent workflow.
